A Public Service Announcement

WordPress
is officially dead.

You are reading a 5-page static website that was generated, in its entirety, from a single sentence typed into Claude. No themes. No plugins. No wp-admin. No update nag. No PHP fatal error at 2am.

Read the Manifesto → See The Proof
The Three Laws Of The Post-CMS Era

Themes are over.
Plugins are over.
Prompts are forever.

The 20-year reign of the bloated content management system has ended. Not with a security patch, but with a chat box.

01

No database

This site does not have a database. It does not need one. There is nothing to backup, nothing to migrate, nothing to optimize at midnight.

02

No login screen

There is no /wp-login.php for bots to hammer. The admin panel is a text input, and it lives in your terminal.

03

No update Tuesday

You will not be paged because Yoast SEO and Elementor disagreed about a function name. They have both been retired.

04

No theme shop

You stopped paying $79 for a "premium" theme that ships 14 sliders and one CSS file written by a stranger in 2014.

"Why install a CMS to publish five pages
when five pages is the entire job?"

— every developer, the moment they tried this once

What Just Happened

One prompt. Five pages. Zero dependencies.

The user typed a single request. Claude returned every file you are currently looking at — HTML, CSS, navigation, copy, tone of voice, the works. It took less time than logging into a hosting dashboard.

View the receipt →
© 2026 The Prompt Liberation Front. Hand-served by your filesystem.
